stories.title: Pistons Complete Historic 3-1 Comeback — Cade 32/12, Harris 30, First Series Win Since 2008

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
DOWN 3-1. DIDN'T MATTER.

Detroit became the 15th team in NBA history to rally from a 3-1 deficit. First Pistons playoff series win in 18 years. This team is DIFFERENT.

#HoopHeroes #Pistons #NBAPlayoffs

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Cade Cunningham 32 pts/12 ast. Tobias Harris 30 pts (career playoff-high).

First Detroit teammates with 30+ in a playoff game since 1977. The new Big Two is REAL.

#HoopHeroes #Pistons #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
The Detroit Pistons are going to the second round of the NBA Playoffs for the first time since 2008 — and they did it in the most dramatic fashion imaginable.

Down 3-1 to the Orlando Magic, with their season seemingly over, Detroit won three consecutive elimination games to complete one of the rarest comebacks in NBA history. Only 14 other teams had ever done it before. On Sunday, the Pistons made it 15 with a dominant 116-94 rout in Game 7.

Tobias Harris was the unexpected hero, pouring in a playoff career-high 30 points on 11-of-18 shooting, including five three-pointers, adding nine rebounds and three steals. Then Cade Cunningham took over with 32 points and 12 assists — joining Isiah Thomas as the only Pistons in franchise history with 20-plus points and 10-plus assists in a Game 7. Together they became the first Detroit teammates to each score 30 points in a playoff game since Bob Lanier and Howard Porter in 1977.

Paolo Banchero gave everything he had — 38 points on the losing end — but the rest of the Magic shot just 10-of-37 from the field. Cade and Harris were simply too much.

The Pistons now face the Cavaliers in Round 2, starting Tuesday in Detroit. After 18 years, the city of Detroit has a playoff team worth believing in again.
facebook.image_caption: CADE. HARRIS. PISTONS. ROUND 2. Detroit completes the comeback for the ages — first series win since 2008. What a moment for this city.

article.headline: Cade and Harris Power Detroit's Historic Comeback — Pistons Advance for First Time Since 2008

stories.title: Allen 22/19 Powers Cavaliers Past Raptors 114-102 in Game 7 — East Semis Set

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
JARRETT ALLEN. 22 POINTS. 19 REBOUNDS.

He put the Cavs on his back in Q3 — 10 boards, 5 offensive, outscoring Toronto 38-19 in the quarter. Unsung hero of the playoffs.

#HoopHeroes #Cavaliers #NBAPlayoffs

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Cavs 114, Raptors 102. Cleveland punches its ticket to Round 2.

The No. 4 seed heads to Detroit to face No. 1 Pistons. Two Game 7 warriors. One Tuesday night showdown.

#HoopHeroes #Cavaliers #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
Jarrett Allen is not going to make a highlight reel you'll see on SportsCenter. He doesn't dunk over people or drain half-court shots. But on Sunday night, he was the single most important player on the court.

Allen finished with 22 points and 19 rebounds in Cleveland's 114-102 Game 7 win over the Toronto Raptors — but the numbers don't tell the full story. It was his third quarter that decided this series. With the Cavaliers needing a momentum shift after trailing by 10 in the second quarter, Allen grabbed 10 rebounds — five of them offensive — as Cleveland outscored Toronto 38-19 in the period to take complete control.

Donovan Mitchell added 22 points and James Harden contributed 18. Scottie Barnes put up 24 for the Raptors in a valiant losing effort. But once Cleveland took that lead, they never let it go — building it to 19 in the third and 22 in the fourth.

The Cavaliers advance as the No. 4 seed to face the No. 1 Detroit Pistons, who won their own Game 7 earlier in the day. Both teams are coming off emotional, draining wins — and it sets up one of the most intriguing Eastern Conference Semifinals in years. Game 1 is Tuesday night in Detroit at 7 p.m. ET.
facebook.image_caption: 22 points. 19 rebounds. That's JARRETT ALLEN in Game 7. Cavaliers advance to Round 2 to face the Pistons. This East bracket is loaded.

article.headline: Allen's 22 Points and 19 Rebounds Carry Cavaliers to Game 7 Victory, East Semifinals Await

stories.title: Second Round Opens Tonight — Knicks vs. 76ers G1 at MSG, 8PM ET; Embiid Probable

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text:
MSG is READY. Knicks vs. 76ers — Round 2, Game 1. 8PM ET tonight.

Jalen Brunson vs. Joel Embiid (probable). Two teams that both won series from the brink. Who survives Night 1?

#HoopHeroes #Knicks #NBAPlayoffs

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text:
Joel Embiid is PROBABLE tonight at MSG. After an appendectomy. After eliminating the Celtics from a 1-3 hole.

The man does not quit. Knicks-76ers G1 8PM ET.

#HoopHeroes #76ers #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
The second round of the 2026 NBA Playoffs kicks off tonight — and it opens at the most iconic arena in basketball.

Madison Square Garden will host Game 1 of Knicks versus 76ers at 8 p.m. ET, and the story lines practically write themselves. New York's Jalen Brunson, who has been the heartbeat of this Knicks team all spring, averaged 26.3 points through the first round alongside Karl-Anthony Towns' 18.7. Philadelphia's Joel Embiid is listed as probable despite a right hip contusion — and let's remember, he had his appendix removed during this postseason before willing the 76ers back from a 1-3 deficit against the Celtics.

Embiid averaged 28 points, nine rebounds, and seven assists in his playoff games. When healthy, he changes everything.

This is a heavyweight matchup between two teams that know what it means to fight back. The Knicks swept through Atlanta in six games, including the most dominant single-game performance of the playoffs — a 47-point halftime lead in Game 6 that still doesn't seem real. The 76ers needed seven games to put away the Celtics. Both paths here required something special.

The Garden will be electric tonight. The NBA second round has officially arrived.
facebook.image_caption: Round 2 starts TONIGHT. Knicks vs. 76ers, Game 1 — Madison Square Garden, 8PM ET. Brunson. Embiid. The Garden. It doesn't get better than this.

article.headline: The Garden Is Ready: Knicks-76ers Round 2 Begins Tonight, Embiid Probable at 8PM ET

stories.title: West Semis Preview — Spurs-Wolves G1 Tonight 9:30PM ET, Thunder-Lakers G1 Tue 8:30PM ET

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Round 2 is HERE. Tonight:
• Spurs vs. Wolves G1, 9:30PM ET — Wembanyama vs. Edwards

Tuesday:
• Thunder vs. Lakers G1, 8:30PM ET — SGA vs. LeBron

Greatest week of basketball all year.

#HoopHeroes #NBAPlayoffs

facebook.long_form:
The NBA's second round doesn't just start tonight on the East — the West is getting in on the action too.

Game 1 of Spurs versus Timberwolves tips off at 9:30 p.m. ET from San Antonio, giving us a first look at the matchup everyone has been waiting for: Victor Wembanyama going up against a Minnesota squad that beat Denver without Anthony Edwards and Dillon DiVincenzo in Round 1. The Wolves showed incredible depth when it counted most. Wembanyama averaged 17 points and 14 rebounds in the Spurs' five-game win over Portland. De'Aaron Fox added 21 points per game. This is a genuinely open series.

Then on Tuesday, the most anticipated Western matchup opens: the No. 1 Oklahoma City Thunder versus the Los Angeles Lakers. SGA versus LeBron. Holmgren versus Davis. The Lakers are 16-point underdogs in Game 1 at OKC — and that is exactly the kind of situation LeBron James has historically turned into something memorable.

Four second-round series. Eight teams. Starting tonight and running through the week. It's the best stretch in the basketball calendar.
facebook.image_caption: The Western Conference second round starts TONIGHT. Spurs-Wolves at 9:30PM ET — Wembanyama vs. Minnesota. Then Thunder-Lakers Tuesday. Buckle up.

article.headline: Western Conference Semifinals Are Here: Wembanyama-Edwards Tonight, SGA-LeBron on Tuesday

stories.title: Cavs vs. Pistons Series Preview — Two Game 7 Warriors Meet in Round 2 Starting Tuesday

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Cavs vs. Pistons. No. 4 visits No. 1. Two teams that just came off emotional Game 7 wins.

Mitchell. Mobley. Allen vs. Cade. Harris. Duren. Game 1 Tuesday in Detroit 7PM ET. Must-watch.

#HoopHeroes #Cavaliers #Pistons

facebook.long_form:
Two teams. Two Game 7 wins. Same Sunday. Now they play each other.

The Cleveland Cavaliers and Detroit Pistons both punched their Round 2 tickets on May 3 in the most dramatic way possible — and they meet in the Eastern Conference Semifinals starting Tuesday night in Detroit at 7 p.m. ET on Peacock/NBC.

The Pistons are the No. 1 seed for a reason — Cade Cunningham posted 32 points and 12 assists in his Game 7, Tobias Harris added 30, and Jalen Duren is one of the most physical young big men in the East. At home in Detroit, where the city's basketball energy hasn't been this alive in nearly two decades, the Pistons have every advantage.

But the Cavaliers are a battle-tested unit that has overcome adversity at every turn. Donovan Mitchell delivers in clutch moments. Evan Mobley is a legitimate two-way star. And Jarrett Allen just reminded everyone — with his 22-point, 19-rebound Game 7 — that when he's locked in, he changes what defenses can do against Cleveland.

Game 1 is Tuesday, May 5, at Little Caesars Arena. Don't be late.
facebook.image_caption: Two Game 7 warriors. One second-round series. Cavs at Pistons — Game 1 Tuesday 7PM ET. After Sunday's drama, this should be appointment viewing.

article.headline: Both Needed a Game 7 to Get Here: Cavaliers vs. Pistons Is the Eastern Conference's Most Compelling Semifinal
